reasoning: The ECB's deposit facility rate on October 30, 2025 was 2.00%, down from 3.75% in mid-2024, indicating a steady rate-cutting cycle. Central banks extremely rarely pivot from cutting to hiking without a prolonged pause. Furthermore, looking at the standard ECB Governing Council schedule, there are typically no monetary policy meetings in November, and December meetings invariably occur on the second or third Thursday of the month (Dec 11 or Dec 18 in 2025). Even if it occurred on the first Thursday (Dec 4), rate changes usually take effect the following Wednesday (Dec 10), which bypasses the December 9 resolution date. Therefore, for Nov 16 and Dec 9, there is virtually zero chance of a rate hike. For the Feb 7, 2026 resolution date, a late-January meeting will have occurred, making a rate change mechanically possible, but a hike in the midst of an easing cycle down to 2.00% remains highly unlikely. Probabilities are kept very low and increase slightly over time to reflect the expanding, though still negligible, window for a surprise hawkish pivot.
